title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption:
National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration

The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.

This choropleth map displays the status of national migration policies across the globe for the year 2021, utilizing a five-tier color scale to categorize performance. The legend defines the spectrum from red, indicating countries that "Require further progress," through orange for those that "Partially meet criteria," light blue for nations that "Meet criteria," and dark blue for those that "Fully meet criteria," with grey hatching denoting areas where "No data" is available. The visual distribution shows that the majority of nations fall into the light blue category, signifying that they meet the established criteria. This grouping includes the United States, Canada, Brazil, Australia, Russia, and most of Western and Northern Europe. A smaller set of countries appears in the dark blue band, fully meeting the criteria, visible in Peru, South Africa, and Iran. Conversely, the orange band, representing partial compliance, covers large geographic swaths including China, India, Mexico, and much of the Middle East and North Africa. The red category, indicating the lowest level of alignment, is visible in specific nations such as Yemen, Myanmar, and Chad. Significant portions of the map, particularly across central Africa and Greenland, remain hatched in grey, showing an absence of reported data. The data presents a global landscape where full compliance is the exception rather than the rule, with most countries clustered in the middle tiers of the scale.
